Daily Pre-IPO Briefing

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VV_bNz6-NsvnW2LsmH21lSqjlW2C4PM95PzVdQN8XrLFz3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3pNW2zCJLc8_LsgCW35Xnbq2L-dP0W3skBPg48wYy2W5kHpZH7NmQvkVtxdQN4Ydm0PW5yWX2P1X2Z3yW1vLljd4Hdnz9W8fD_dP6M7XztW4fcCtk8MN9W9W69fwtq7Jvkr3W3KBnD65tpjZSW1JWhLx1FmYQ5W11wjYH98X0GhW49hy-R4tK2L7W7f2CPT2qcrJ8N4b5tKGqwcPGW3z0m415-cbJbN145Y_VVN0KBW6qjVHs2KFD_8W9hDccH2WMGN-W1T7kH-10mzcpW6Ftysm899PJHf6pG7jd04 )

Daily Pre-IPO Briefing (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VV_bNz6-NsvnW2LsmH21lSqjlW2C4PM95PzVdQN8XrLFz3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3pNW2zCJLc8_LsgCW35Xnbq2L-dP0W3skBPg48wYy2W5kHpZH7NmQvkVtxdQN4Ydm0PW5yWX2P1X2Z3yW1vLljd4Hdnz9W8fD_dP6M7XztW4fcCtk8MN9W9W69fwtq7Jvkr3W3KBnD65tpjZSW1JWhLx1FmYQ5W11wjYH98X0GhW49hy-R4tK2L7W7f2CPT2qcrJ8N4b5tKGqwcPGW3z0m415-cbJbN145Y_VVN0KBW6qjVHs2KFD_8W9hDccH2WMGN-W1T7kH-10mzcpW6Ftysm899PJHf6pG7jd04 )

Hi Oleg,

Welcome back to our Daily Pre-IPO Briefing, your quick recap of major headlines involving companies our investors already have exposure to via StartEngine Private.

Today’s theme? Wall Street is preparing for two potential “blockbuster” IPOs:

- OpenAI is expected to file for an IPO “very soon.” In the meantime, major U.S. index and mutual funds are setting aside cash and preparing to offload existing holdings as they anticipate adding a potential “blockbuster IPO” to their portfolios.¹
- SpaceX has fueled investor enthusiasm for the broader space tech industry with its IPO filing last week. Space and satellite-related stocks “soared” yesterday, including notable jumps for Redwire Corp. (+31%), AST SpaceMobile Inc. (+20%), and Firefly Aerospace Inc. (+21%).²
- Kraken launched Bitcoin Vaults, allowing users to earn up to 2.5% APY from their Bitcoin holdings. Risk and strategy are managed by institutional DeFi firm Sentora, which builds and executes lending and borrowing strategies to earn yield directly on-chain.³

A confidential filing does not guarantee that an IPO will occur. These reports don't imply specific results for investors with exposure to these companies on StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. There's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow, or that OpenAI will be profitable. See risks on the offering page.

Whether it’s AI, crypto infrastructure, or space tech, institutional capital seems increasingly focused on the companies shaping what could come next.

Connect with our team to discuss the latest trends, or explore open current pre-IPO offerings

Explore Pre-IPO Opportunities
(https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VV_bNz6-NsvnW2LsmH21lSqjlW2C4PM95PzVdQN8XrLFz3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3pNW2zCJLc8_LsgCW35Xnbq2L-dP0W3skBPg48wYy2W5kHpZH7NmQvkVtxdQN4Ydm0PW5yWX2P1X2Z3yW1vLljd4Hdnz9W8fD_dP6M7XztW4fcCtk8MN9W9W69fwtq7Jvkr3W3KBnD65tpjZSW1JWhLx1FmYQ5W11wjYH98X0GhW49hy-R4tK2L7W7f2CPT2qcrJ8N4b5tKGqwcPGW3z0m415-cbJbN145Y_VVN0KBW6qjVHs2KFD_8W9hDccH2WM

73689.body:

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/MXjG3X3hGm6W4DRCLj1bs_BVW9fbdmV5PzPg2N2SX24q3pyd0W7Y8-PT6lZ3mvW7ddnWS5RdlQwW2WD4R019r4R8Vly5bl8sn99sN36VvVsHLBF0W1ZC2WH8Jy5p2W89mTL77wQM5FW42c3Gz5rhm6wN45zFLqjTqkhW7vqZ1j4zFRW5W1dZcr48TX7FkW4kg3Jk3rqBnwW4l44jG3N-f1bW7f8LWr7d00RJW8XvBRG4q4lb4W2hyL1W8fd3mgW7wNghY6mVvnjW2P078C29847TW59nHCG22MBLZW736D6_5Wq2CCW3B8vzb6PdXDYW3v8Nnf84X_99N1jlDjzNg7RTW6cBBrD7zBcKXW2_fZwc8YTH4PW7dP79K1V--hgW7HSBzJ8kfjNCf2X2CGz04 )

Hi Oleg,

We just had a pretty amazing week for StartEngine Private.

In fact, it was our third-highest week for investor commitments since launching in Q3 2023.

And one company in particular kept making news: OpenAI.

One of the most coveted companies on our platform delivered a trifecta of major headlines:

- Defeated Elon Musk in a major courtroom victory.¹
- Preparing to file for an IPO in the coming weeks.²
- Beat Anthropic’s Q1 revenue by about $1 billion.³

This paints a pretty clear picture: OpenAI is scaling aggressively for the next phase of AI growth.

That includes an infrastructure deal with “AI factory” Crusoe,⁴ as well as raising $122 billion from Amazon, NVIDIA, SoftBank, and more in “Silicon Valley’s largest-ever funding round.”⁵

A confidential filing does not guarantee that an IPO will occur. These reports don't imply specific results for investors with exposure to OpenAI on StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. There's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow, or that OpenAI will be profitable. 73274.subject: Svadobná výzdoba
73274.body:
Zdravíme nevestu💍🤍,

Ďakujeme veľmi pekne za vašu správu.. Termín svadby máme ešte voľný a veľmi
radi budeme súčasťou vašej svadby.🤍

Spravidla sa s klientami stretávame osobne v showroome v Rozhanovciach
(kúsok od Košíc) kde si spolu vieme vyskladať svadobnú výzdobu a na základe
toho vytvoriť cenovú ponuku na mieru pre vás ak by ste mali záujem prosím
navrhnite deň a čas kedy by vám to najviac vyhovovalo aby sa časovo radi
prispôsobíme vášmu času. Poprípade, ak sme mimo Košíc či Prešova, radi si s
Vami dohodneme videohovor. Prosím navrhnite deň a čas, ktorý by Vám
vyhovoval najviac, pokojne aj cez víkend aj vo večerných hodinách. 🙂
Najbližsie voľne termíny máme ešte aj tento týždeň. Forme stretnutia sa
radi prispôsobíme Vám, čo je pre Vás praktickejšie ☺️

Budeme sa tešiť na prípadnú spoluprácu s Vami.

S pozdravom

Lenka Gattiker,
Vaša osobná posh asistentka

73231.subject: From a Cornell Lab to BlackRock. The Avalanche Story. | One Block Weekly #11
73231.body:
Hello,

Welcome back to One Block Weekly. One coin, one story.

While most blockchains were still arguing about speed, one quietly went and built a different kind of network altogether.

🏔️ Avalanche (AVAX)

Emin Gun Sirer is a Cornell University computer science professor who had been researching distributed systems long before most people knew what a blockchain was.

In 2018, his team published a whitepaper introducing a new consensus mechanism, a way for a network to agree on what is true, that was faster and more scalable than anything Bitcoin or Ethereum used. They called it Avalanche.

Ava Labs was founded to build a blockchain around it. Avalanche launched its mainnet in September 2020, raised $230 million in a public token sale in 48 hours, and immediately positioned itself as the platform serious institutions would eventually choose.

The pitch was simple: all the programmability of Ethereum, none of the congestion.

⚡ A Few Moments That Shaped Avalanche

2021 - AVAX surged over 2,700% in a single year, hitting an all-time high of around $147. Developers flooded in, and Avalanche briefly became the fourth largest blockchain by total value locked.

2022 - Avalanche partnered with Deloitte to build a disaster relief reimbursement platform for US government agencies. A blockchain landing a Big Four accounting firm and a government contract in the same deal was a first.

2023 - The subnet model gained traction. Major institutions, including financial firms and gaming companies, began launching their own custom blockchains, called subnets, built on top of Avalanche infrastructure.

2024 - BlackRock and other asset managers began tokenising real-world assets on Avalanche, bringing government bonds and funds onto the blockchain. Institutional adoption had moved from talk to action.

🔢 What Makes It Different

Avalanche finalises transactions in under two seconds, faster than almost any major blockchain. But the more interesting feature is subnets: custom blockchains that can be spun up within the Avalanche ecosystem, each with its own rules, validators, and token economics.

For institutions that want the benefits of blockchain without sharing space with memecoins and DeFi protocols, that is a compelling offer.

🤯 Three Weird Avalanche Facts

The founder once publicly exposed a flaw in a rival blockchain. Emin Gun Sirer identified and disclosed a critical vulnerability in Bitcoin's network years before Avalanche existed. He is one of the few people in crypto who arrived with an academic reputation already intact.

A major gaming subnet launched and went quiet. One of the highest profile early subnets was built for a blockchain gaming ecosystem. It launched with significant fanfare and struggled to find its audience, becoming an early lesson in the gap between institutional infrastructure and consumer adoption.

The whitepaper was published anonymously. The original Avalanche consensus paper was released und

73054.subject: BAVA Is Live: Avalanche Just Got a Seat at the Institutional Table
73054.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/bava-is-live-avalanche-just-got-a

Getting exposure to AVAX used to mean managing wallets, figuring out private keys, and navigating staking mechanics on your own. For most institutional investors and everyday brokerage users, that friction was enough to keep them on the sidelines.
Bitwise Asset Management launched the Bitwise Avalanche ETF (NYSE: BAVA), giving investors direct exposure to AVAX through a standard brokerage account. No wallet setup. No staking complexity. Also, BAVA actively stakes the fund’s AVAX holdings, aiming to pass those staking rewards back to shareholders.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
What Is BAVA
BAVA is a spot Avalanche ETF, meaning the fund holds actual AVAX tokens rather than derivatives or futures. Bitwise, which manages $11 billion in client assets, runs the product through its in-house staking division, Bitwise Onchain Solutions.
Here is how the staking structure works: roughly 70% of the fund’s AVAX [ https://substack.com/redirect/6d85e6f4-5177-450f-acfe-d09f7cf0257a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] is staked at any given time, targeting Avalanche’s current average staking reward rate of 5.4% [ https://substack.com/redirect/503f0339-b361-4e87-9cc7-c57861679e02?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]. The remaining 30% [ https://substack.com/redirect/6d85e6f4-5177-450f-acfe-d09f7cf0257a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] stays in a liquidity reserve to handle redemptions. Net rewards flow back to shareholders on a regular schedule.
The fund trades on the NYSE under the ticker BAVA. The sponsor fee is 0.34% [ https://substack.com/redirect/53be78a2-ae7e-472c-97bc-a8ece1096053?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], waived entirely for the first month on the fund’s first $500 million in assets. Early trading showed $400,000 in volume within the first 90 minutes, which Bloomberg ETF analyst James Seyffart described as quite good for a launch.
Why Avalanche, Why Now
The timing of BAVA reflects something that has been building quietly in the Avalanche ecosystem, a growing list of real-world assets deployments that go well beyond crypto-native use cases. Currently, Avalanche is emerging as one of the leading platforms for businesses, governments, and real-world use cases by powering institutional projects like:
FIFA [ https://substack.com/redirect/c81eaf17-5d80-488f-9fbe-4f420c61784e?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]‘s proprietary blockchain for digital collectibles, including ticket options for the 2026 World Cup
Wyoming’s Frontier (FRNT [ https://substack.com/redirect/0a3bb75b-c123-4bdb-a0cb-fa27587f5381?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]) Stable Token, the first stat
